heading: Definitions
label: 11360 Definitions

HEARTH Act Technical Corrections, , , provided that:Pub. L. 112–141, div. F, title II, § 100261126 Stat. 978July 6, 2012“For purposes of title IV of the McKinney-Vento Homeless Assistance Act ( et seq.)—42 U.S.C. 11360“(1)the term ‘local government’ includes an instrumentality of a unit of general purpose local government other than a public housing agency that is established pursuant to legislation and designated by the chief executive to act on behalf of the local government with regard to activities funded under such title IV and includes a combination of general purpose local governments, such as an association of governments, that is recognized by the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development;“(2)the term ‘State’ includes any instrumentality of any of the several States designated by the Governor to act on behalf of the State and does not include the District of Columbia;“(3)for purposes of environmental review, the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development shall continue to permit assistance and projects to be treated as assistance for special projects that are subject to section 305(c) of the Multifamily Housing Property Disposition Reform Act of 1994 (), and subject to the regulations issued by the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to implement such section; and42 U.S.C. 3547“(4)a metropolitan city and an urban county that each receive an allocation under such title IV and are located within a geographic area that is covered by a single continuum of care may jointly request the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to permit the urban county or the metropolitan city, as agreed to by such county and city, to receive and administer their combined allocations under a single grant.”

DefinitionsFor provisions relating to definitions of “State” and “local government” as used in this section, see , set out as a HEARTH Act Technical Corrections note above.section 100261 of Pub. L. 112–141

Termination of Trust Territory of the Pacific IslandsFor termination of Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands, see note set out preceding , Territories and Insular Possessions.section 1681 of Title 48

HEARTH Act Technical Corrections, , , provided that:Pub. L. 112–141, div. F, title II, § 100261126 Stat. 978July 6, 2012“For purposes of title IV of the McKinney-Vento Homeless Assistance Act ( et seq.)—42 U.S.C. 11360“(1)the term ‘local government’ includes an instrumentality of a unit of general purpose local government other than a public housing agency that is established pursuant to legislation and designated by the chief executive to act on behalf of the local government with regard to activities funded under such title IV and includes a combination of general purpose local governments, such as an association of governments, that is recognized by the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development;“(2)the term ‘State’ includes any instrumentality of any of the several States designated by the Governor to act on behalf of the State and does not include the District of Columbia;“(3)for purposes of environmental review, the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development shall continue to permit assistance and projects to be treated as assistance for special projects that are subject to section 305(c) of the Multifamily Housing Property Disposition Reform Act of 1994 (), and subject to the regulations issued by the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to implement such section; and42 U.S.C. 3547“(4)a metropolitan city and an urban county that each receive an allocation under such title IV and are located within a geographic area that is covered by a single continuum of care may jointly request the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to permit the urban county or the metropolitan city, as agreed to by such county and city, to receive and administer their combined allocations under a single grant.”

children.label: A
children.text: has income below 30 percent of median income for the geographic area;

children.label: B
children.text: has insufficient resources immediately available to attain housing stability; and

children.label: i
children.text: has moved frequently because of economic reasons;

children.label: ii
children.text: is living in the home of another because of economic hardship;

children.label: iii
children.text: has been notified that their right to occupy their current housing or living situation will be terminated;

children.label: iv
children.text: lives in a hotel or motel;

children.label: v
children.text: lives in severely overcrowded housing;

children.label: vi
children.text: is exiting an institution; or

children.label: vii
children.text: otherwise lives in housing that has characteristics associated with instability and an increased risk of homelessness.

children.label: i
children.text: is homeless and lives or resides in a place not meant for human habitation, a safe haven, or in an emergency shelter;

children.label: ii
children.text: has been homeless and living or residing in a place not meant for human habitation, a safe haven, or in an emergency shelter continuously for at least 1 year or on at least 4 separate occasions in the last 3 years; and

children.label: iii
children.text: has an adult head of household (or a minor head of household if no adult is present in the household) with a diagnosable substance use disorder, serious mental illness, developmental disability (as defined in ), post traumatic stress disorder, cognitive impairments resulting from a brain injury, or chronic physical illness or disability, including the co-occurrence of 2 or more of those conditions.section 15002 of this title

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: B Rule of construction
children.text: A person who currently lives or resides in an institutional care facility, including a jail, substance abuse or mental health treatment facility, hospital or other similar facility, and has resided there for fewer than 90 days shall be considered chronically homeless if such person met all of the requirements described in subparagraph (A) prior to entering that facility.

children.label: A
children.text: carries out the duties specified in ;section 11360a of this title

children.label: B
children.text: serves as the applicant for project sponsors who jointly submit a single application for a grant under part C in accordance with a collaborative process; and

children.label: C
children.text: if the entity is a legal entity and is awarded such grant, receives such grant directly from the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: satisfies ; andsection 11382 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: is submitted to the Secretary by a collaborative applicant.

children.heading: Consolidated Plan
children.label: 5 Consolidated Plan
children.text: The term “Consolidated Plan” means a comprehensive housing affordability strategy and community development plan required in part 91 of title 24, Code of Federal Regulations.

children.heading: Eligible entity
children.label: 6 Eligible entity
children.text: The term “eligible entity” means, with respect to a part, a public entity, a private entity, or an entity that is a combination of public and private entities, that is eligible to directly receive grant amounts under such part.

children.heading: Families with children and youth defined as homeless under other Federal statutes
children.label: 7 Families with children and youth defined as homeless under other Federal statutes
children.text: The term “families with children and youth defined as homeless under other Federal statutes” means any children or youth that are defined as “homeless” under any Federal statute other than this part, but are not defined as homeless under , and shall also include the parent, parents, or guardian of such children or youth under part B of subchapter VI this chapter ( et seq.).section 11302 of this title142 U.S.C. 11431So in original. Probably should be “of this”.1

children.heading: Formula area
children.label: 8 Formula area
children.text: The term “formula area” has the meaning given the term in section 1000.302 of title 24, Code of Federal Regulations, or any successor regulation.

children.heading: Geographic area
children.label: 9 Geographic area
children.text: The term “geographic area” means a State, metropolitan city, urban county, town, village, or other nonentitlement area, a formula area, or a combination or consortia of such, in the United States, as described in .section 5306 of this title

children.label: I
children.text: is expected to be long-continuing or of indefinite duration;

children.label: II
children.text: substantially impedes the individual’s ability to live independently;

children.label: III
children.text: could be improved by the provision of more suitable housing conditions; and

children.label: IV
children.text: is a physical, mental, or emotional impairment, including an impairment caused by alcohol or drug abuse, post traumatic stress disorder, or brain injury;

children.label: ii
children.text: is a developmental disability, as defined in ; orsection 15002 of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: is the disease of acquired immunodeficiency syndrome or any condition arising from the etiologic agency for acquired immunodeficiency syndrome.

children.heading: Rule
children.label: B Rule
children.text: Nothing in clause (iii) of subparagraph (A) shall be construed to limit eligibility under clause (i) or (ii) of subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Indian Tribe
children.label: 11 Indian Tribe
children.text: The term “Indian Tribe” has the meaning given the term “Indian tribe” in .section 4103 of title 25

children.label: A
children.text: an entity described in and exempt from tax under section 501(a) of such title;section 501(c)(3) of title 26

children.label: B
children.text: an instrumentality of State or local government; or

children.label: C
children.text: a consortium of instrumentalities of State or local governments that has constituted itself as an entity.

children.heading: Metropolitan city; urban county; nonentitlement area
children.label: 13 Metropolitan city; urban county; nonentitlement area
children.text: The terms “metropolitan city”, “urban county”, and “nonentitlement area” have the meanings given such terms in .section 5302(a) of this title

children.heading: New
children.label: 14 New
children.text: The term “new” means, with respect to housing, that no assistance has been provided under this subchapter for the housing.

children.label: A
children.text: the administration, maintenance, repair, and security of such housing;

children.label: B
children.text: utilities, fuel, furnishings, and equipment for such housing; or

children.label: C
children.text: coordination of services as needed to ensure long-term housing stability.

children.heading: Outpatient health services
children.label: 16 Outpatient health services
children.text: The term “outpatient health services” means outpatient health care services, mental health services, and outpatient substance abuse services.

children.heading: Permanent housing
children.label: 17 Permanent housing
children.text: The term “permanent housing” means community-based housing without a designated length of stay, and includes both permanent supportive housing and permanent housing without supportive services.

children.label: A
children.text: a first and last name;

children.label: B
children.text: a home or other physical address;

children.label: C
children.text: contact information (including a postal, e-mail or Internet protocol address, or telephone or facsimile number);

children.label: D
children.text: a social security number; and

children.label: E
children.text: any other information, including date of birth, racial or ethnic background, or religious affiliation, that, in combination with any other non-personally identifying information, would serve to identify any individual.

children.label: A
children.text: no part of the net earnings of which inures to the benefit of any member, founder, contributor, or individual;

children.label: B
children.text: that has a voluntary board;

children.label: C
children.text: that has an accounting system, or has designated a fiscal agent in accordance with requirements established by the Secretary; and

children.label: D
children.text: that practices nondiscrimination in the provision of assistance.

children.heading: Project
children.label: 20 Project
children.text: The term “project” means, with respect to activities carried out under part C, eligible activities described in , undertaken pursuant to a specific endeavor, such as serving a particular population or providing a particular resource.section 11383(a) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the recipient or a project sponsor; and

children.label: ii
children.text: an owner of a structure that exists as of the date the contract is entered into; and

children.label: B
children.text: provides that rental assistance payments shall be made to the owner and that the units in the structure shall be occupied by eligible persons for not less than the term of the contract.

children.heading: Project sponsor
children.label: 22 Project sponsor
children.text: The term “project sponsor” means, with respect to proposed eligible activities, the organization directly responsible for carrying out the proposed eligible activities.

children.label: A
children.text: submits an application for a grant under that is approved by the Secretary;section 11382 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: receives the grant directly from the Secretary to support approved projects described in the application; and

children.label: i
children.text: serves as a project sponsor for the projects; or

children.label: ii
children.text: awards the funds to project sponsors to carry out the projects.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 24 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development.

children.heading: Serious mental illness
children.label: 25 Serious mental illness
children.text: The term “serious mental illness” means a severe and persistent mental illness or emotional impairment that seriously limits a person’s ability to live independently.

children.heading: Solo applicant
children.label: 26 Solo applicant
children.text: The term “solo applicant” means an entity that is an eligible entity, directly submits an application for a grant under part C to the Secretary, and, if awarded such grant, receives such grant directly from the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: the recipient or a project sponsor; and

children.label: I
children.text: is a private organization; and

children.label: II
children.text: owns or leases dwelling units; and

children.label: B
children.text: provides that rental assistance payments shall be made to the independent entity and that eligible persons shall occupy such assisted units.

children.heading: State
children.label: 28 State
children.text: Except as used in part B, the term “State” means each of the several States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the United States Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, the Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands, and any other territory or possession of the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: the establishment and operation of a child care services program for families experiencing homelessness;

children.label: B
children.text: the establishment and operation of an employment assistance program, including providing job training;

children.label: C
children.text: the provision of outpatient health services, food, and case management;

children.label: D
children.text: the provision of assistance in obtaining permanent housing, employment counseling, and nutritional counseling;

children.label: E
children.text: the provision of outreach services, advocacy, life skills training, and housing search and counseling services;

children.label: F
children.text: the provision of mental health services, trauma counseling, and victim services;

children.label: G
children.text: the provision of assistance in obtaining other Federal, State, and local assistance available for residents of supportive housing (including mental health benefits, employment counseling, and medical assistance, but not including major medical equipment);

children.label: H
children.text: the provision of legal services for purposes including requesting reconsiderations and appeals of veterans and public benefit claim denials and resolving outstanding warrants that interfere with an individual’s ability to obtain and retain housing;

children.label: i
children.text: transportation services that facilitate an individual’s ability to obtain and maintain employment; and

children.label: ii
children.text: health care; and

children.label: J
children.text: other supportive services necessary to obtain and maintain housing.

children.label: i
children.text: in a particular structure or unit for not more than the first year of the participation;

children.label: ii
children.text: within a particular geographic area for the full period of the participation, or the period remaining after the period referred to in subparagraph (A); and

children.label: B
children.text: provides that a person may receive such assistance and move to another structure, unit, or geographic area if the person has complied with all other obligations of the program and has moved out of the assisted dwelling unit in order to protect the health or safety of an individual who is or has been the victim of domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, or stalking, and who reasonably believed he or she was imminently threatened by harm from further violence if he or she remained in the assisted dwelling unit.

children.heading: Transitional housing
children.label: 31 Transitional housing
children.text: The term “transitional housing” means housing the purpose of which is to facilitate the movement of individuals and families experiencing homelessness to permanent housing within 24 months or such longer period as the Secretary determines necessary.

children.heading: Unified funding agency
children.label: 32 Unified funding agency
children.text: The term “unified funding agency” means a collaborative applicant that performs the duties described in .section 11360a(g) of this title

children.heading: Underserved populations
children.label: 33 Underserved populations
children.text: The term “underserved populations” includes populations underserved because of geographic location, underserved racial and ethnic populations, populations underserved because of special needs (such as language barriers, disabilities, alienage status, or age), and any other population determined to be underserved by the Secretary, as appropriate.

children.heading: Victim service provider
children.label: 34 Victim service provider
children.text: The term “victim service provider” means a private nonprofit organization whose primary mission is to provide services to victims of domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, or stalking. Such term includes rape crisis centers, battered women’s shelters, domestic violence transitional housing programs, and other programs.

children.heading: Victim services
children.label: 35 Victim services
children.text: The term “victim services” means services that assist domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, or stalking victims, including services offered by rape crisis centers and domestic violence shelters, and other organizations, with a documented history of effective work concerning domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, or stalking.
